name: Beef Pita, Greek Style #2
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------
Garnishes:
- Parsley Sprigs
- 12 Cherry Tomatoes
- Fresh Fruit Kabobs (optional)
Combine ground beef, onion, and garlic in a large frying pan. Cook, stirring frequently, until the beef loses its pink color. Pour off drippings. Add mushrooms, bay leaf, salt, chili powder, cumin powder, and cinnamon. Cook, stirring frequently, until mushrooms are tender about 5 minutes. Stir in tomato sauce and wine; cook, covered, for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the bay leaf. Cool while preparing the cheese filling. In a medium bowl, combine egg and cream cheese. Beat with an electric mixer until smooth. Stir in cottage cheese and feta cheese, then blend. Brush melted butter on a 13 x 9-inch baking pan. Line the pan with one sheet of pastry, fitting it to the contour of the pan. Brush the pastry with butter. Layer with three more pastry sheets, brushing each with butter. Sprinkle bread crumbs evenly over the top. Spoon one-fifth of the meat filling into the layer over the crumbs and one-fifth of the cheese filling over the meat. Place one pastry sheet over the cheese filling, crinkling to fit inside the dimensions of the pan; brush with butter and layer with one-fifth of the meat and one-fifth of the cheese fillings. Repeat with three more pastry sheets, crinkling each, brushing with butter, and topping with fillings. Turn the bottom pastry ends up over the filling. Place the remaining eight pastry sheets smoothly over the top, brushing each with butter.
Using a spatula, tuck the top pastry sheets around the inside edges of the pan. With a sharp knife, score the top lightly in half lengthwise and sixths crosswise (do not cut through). Bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 1 hour or until the top is golden brown. Cool at least 10 minutes before cutting along the scored lines. Place a cherry tomato on each of 12 small wooden picks and insert the pick into the center of each serving. Garnish individual servings with parsley and fresh fruit kabobs, if desired.
Fresh Fruit Kabobs: To make fresh fruit kabobs, place chunks of fresh pineapple, cantaloupe, whole strawberries, or other fruits in season on small wooden skewers.
Servings: 12
